expectations.evidence: Appears 10 times throughout response, e.g. line 113 'Nwbfile  # PK FK', line 121 'Nwbfile is a top-level Manual table (common/common_nwbfile.py:45-52)', and in the closure table line 168.
Appears 12 times, e.g. '-> IntervalList # secondary FK' on LFPBandV1 (line 22), LFPSelection (line 69), and discussion of two IntervalList rows required at line 126.
Appears in section 4 ('## 4. LFPElectrodeGroup and below', line 73-85) defining the table, in LFPSelection FK list line 68, and in the closure table.
Section 'LFPSelection (lfp/v1/lfp.py:21-41)' line 64, listed as direct FK parent of LFPV1 line 59, and in the upstream closure table.
Discussed throughout, e.g. line 54 'The standard path (LFPV1) is by far the common case', section 'LFPV1 -> LFPSelection' lines 56-62.
Appears 11 times, with section 3 dedicated to LFPOutput as the merge master ('## 3. LFPOutput and its source tables', line 46), explicitly named as FK parent of LFPBandSelection line 35.

Appears 7 times: as PK FK of LFPBandSelection (line 36), of LFPSelection (line 70), and discussed in section 7 line 128 noting 'You need a row keyed by (filter_name, filter_sampling_rate) matching the upstream LFPs sampling rate' for both LFPV1 and LFPBandV1.
Section 2 dedicated to LFPBandSelection ('## 2. LFPBandSelection parents', line 28-44), declared as direct PK FK of LFPBandV1 line 20.
Subject of the response — title 'Upstream tables required for LFPBandV1.populate(key)' (line 1), section 1 'Direct FK parents of LFPBandV1' (line 14).
Appears 10 times, dedicated section 8 'Runtime-only requirement: Raw' (line 130) explaining LFPV1.make() reads Raw via (Raw & nwbf_key).fetch_nwb().

Section 6 'Session and what it pulls in' (line 109), 'Session -> Nwbfile # PK FK' (line 114), and Session listed as parent of ElectrodeGroup, IntervalList, Raw, LFPElectrodeGroup in the closure table line 167.

expectations.text: Names every upstream table including the parameter (FirFilterParameters) and selection tables (LFPSelection, LFPBandSelection)
expectations.evidence: All three named explicitly with FK definitions: FirFilterParameters as parent of both LFPSelection (line 70) and LFPBandSelection (line 36); LFPSelection as parent of LFPV1 (line 59, 64-71); LFPBandSelection as parent of LFPBandV1 (line 20, 28-44). Closure table (lines 153-172) lists all three as required.

expectations.text: Names the Raw table as the data source fetched inside LFPV1's `make()` body (Raw is not an FK of LFPSelection; it is pulled via `(Raw & key).fetch_nwb()` at make() time and must be present to regenerate LFPV1 from scratch)
expectations.evidence: Section 8 (lines 130-145) states explicitly: 'This one does not show up in any FK graph rooted at LFPBandV1, but it is required for LFPBandV1.populate(key) to succeed via the LFPV1 path: LFPV1.make() reads Raw at runtime' and quotes 'rawdata = (Raw & nwbf_key).fetch_nwb()[0]["raw"]' from lfp/v1/lfp.py:68, 72-74. Also calls out 'Raw is a runtime-fetch handle, not a stored pipeline output' (line 145).

expectations.text: Does not skip the LFPOutput merge wrapper between LFPV1 and LFPBandSelection
expectations.evidence: LFPOutput is named explicitly as the merge master that LFPBandSelection FKs into: 'LFPBandSelection -> LFPOutput.proj(lfp_merge_id=merge_id) # PK FK to LFPOutput merge master' (line 34), with section 3 dedicated to it ('LFPOutput is the LFP merge master (lfp/lfp_merge.py:16-44). For a LFPBandV1 row to populate, the merge entry in LFPOutput has to point to one of its three part-table sources... LFPOutput.LFPV1 -> LFPV1', lines 46-53).

claims.claim: LFPBandV1's direct FK parents are LFPBandSelection, AnalysisNwbfile, IntervalList per lfp/analysis/v1/lfp_band.py:290-297
claims.evidence: Matches the LFPBandV1 definition in src/spyglass/lfp/analysis/v1/lfp_band.py — LFPBandSelection is the PK FK and AnalysisNwbfile + IntervalList are secondary FKs. Line range cited (290-297) is in the right area for the table definition.

claims.claim: LFPBandSelection FKs LFPOutput.proj(lfp_merge_id='merge_id'), FirFilterParameters, IntervalList.proj(target_interval_list_name=...) per lfp_band.py:22-39
claims.evidence: Consistent with the LFPBandSelection definition; the merge_id projection and target_interval_list_name renaming are correct DataJoint patterns for this table, and the part-table LFPBandElectrode FKing LFPElectrodeGroup.LFPElectrode is correctly cited.

claims.claim: LFPV1.make() fetches Raw via (Raw & nwbf_key).fetch_nwb() — runtime dependency not in FK graph
claims.evidence: Direct quote from lfp/v1/lfp.py:68, 72-74. Captures the central insight: Raw is a runtime-fetched dependency, not declared as an FK on any LFP-pipeline table.

claims.claim: FirFilterParameters needs a row at the upstream LFP sampling rate for LFPV1 and a separate row at lfp_sampling_rate for LFPBandV1
claims.evidence: Section 7 (line 128) explains 'set_lfp_band_electrodes derives filter_sampling_rate from the LFPs lfp_sampling_rate, lfp_band.py:120-122, 220'. This correctly distinguishes the two FirFilterParameters keys required at the two pipeline stages, matching the expected_output's note about FirFilterParameters appearing twice.

claims.claim: BrainRegion is a required FK on ElectrodeGroup and Electrode
claims.evidence: Section 5 line 96, 104 lists '-> BrainRegion' as non-nullable on both. The closure table line 172 lists BrainRegion as required.

claims.claim: LFPOutput has three part-table sources: LFPV1, ImportedLFP, CommonLFP
claims.evidence: Lines 50-52 enumerate them; CommonLFP is correctly noted as legacy and rejected by set_lfp_band_electrodes per lfp_band.py:98.

claims.claim: AnalysisNwbfile is auto-created in LFPBandV1.make() (not pre-inserted)
claims.evidence: Line 127: 'AnalysisNwbfile().create(...) (lfp_band.py:500, 584); you dont pre-insert it'. Correct semantics — AnalysisNwbfile is a side-effect of make(), not a precondition row.

suggestions.reason: The substring assertions for `Raw`, `Session`, and `IntervalList` are quite weak — those tokens appear naturally as common DataJoint terms and substrings of longer table names. The semantic assertions (#13, #14) and the consolidated #12 do most of the actual discrimination work; consider whether the 11 substring checks add signal beyond a single 'all of these names appear at least once each' assertion.

suggestions.assertion: Names the Raw table as the data source fetched inside LFPV1's `make()` body
suggestions.reason: Strong and well-targeted — this is the discriminating assertion that catches a model which only walks the FK graph. Worth keeping; just noting it carries most of the eval's value.

eval_feedback.overall: Eval is well-designed for this question. The two semantic assertions (Raw runtime fetch, LFPOutput not skipped) are exactly the right discriminators for distinguishing skill-aware responses from naive FK walks. Substring assertions are redundant but harmless.
